Node.js Basics, Part 18: Template Engines — EJS vs. Pug
A new chapter begins — templates. Templating is a really common technique in development, so this post introduces what it is and compares the two most common engines: EJS and Pug (formerly Jade).
Forgot the Express static file setup for a moment, so here's a quick note on the method.

This post covers something really important — middleware. It's extremely common in Express development, used for things like login guards, 404 pages, and error handling. We'll walk through router.use and a few reusable patterns.
Node.js Basics, Part 16: Express Routing, Query Strings, and URL Params

We've got the basics of Express down, but routing deserves its own post — the concepts here matter a lot for everything that follows. We'll break down a URL's anatomy, set up nested routes, and read query strings and path params.

We've already seen how to start a basic web server and show Hello World, so here we'll go back over the Express code line by line — loading Express, handling routes, starting the server, and reading the port from an env variable.
Node.js Basics, Part 14: Getting Started with the Express Web Framework
Now that we've got the basics of Firebase down, it's time to learn Express. This post covers what Express is and walks through spinning up your first Express app that returns Hello World.
Node.js Basics, Part 13: Firebase Querying — Sorting, Filtering, and Limiting

This post covers the more advanced query syntax — sorting with orderByChild(), filtering with startAt()/endAt()/equalTo(), and limiting results with limitToFirst()/limitToLast(), plus push() as another way to add data.
Node.js Basics, Part 12: Firebase CRUD Basics
Now that the HTML is wired up to Firebase, it's finally time to learn the basics — ref(), set(), child(), remove(), on() and once() — and walk through creating, updating, deleting, and reading data.
Node.js Basics, Part 11: Wiring Up the Firebase SDK
The project's created and the database is ready, so now we'll actually wire the Firebase SDK into our page — and deal with the classic "firebase.database is not a function" error along the way.
Node.js Basics, Part 10: Setting Up a Firebase Realtime Database
We've already created a project, so next up is setting up Firebase's Realtime Database — registering a web app, grabbing the Firebase SDK config, and spinning up the database in test mode.
